lines.english: The mind, the master of the breath, abides in the state of supreme bliss.
then I took my cleansing bath, deep within my self. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਨਖ ਸਿਖ ਇਹੁ ਮਨੁ ਚੀਨੑਾ ॥
lines.translit: jab nakh sikh ihu man cheenaa |
lines.english: When I came to understand this mind, from the tips of my toes to the crown of my head,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਹੀ ਮਿਰਤੁ ਨ ਜਨਮੁ ਜਰਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: nahee mirat na janam jaraa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: There is no death, no re-birth, and no aging for me now. ||1||Pause||
Turning away from materialism, I have found intuitive support.
I have entered into the sky of the mind, and opened the Tenth Gate.
and I have met my Sovereign Lord King without fear. ||2||
My attachment to Maya has been eradicated;
The chakras of the coiled Kundalini energy have been opened,
the moon energy has devoured the sun energy.
When I was focused and merged into the all-pervading Lord,
then the unstruck sound current began to vibrate. ||3||
The Speaker has spoken, and proclaimed the Word of the Shabad.
Says Kabeer, this is the essence. ||4||1||10||
Chanting to the Creator, one crosses over.
The hearer has heard, and enshrined it in the mind.
The moon and the sun are both the embodiment of light.
Within their light, is God, the incomparable. ||1||
O spiritual teacher, contemplate God.
In this light is contained the expanse of the created universe. ||1||Pause||
Says Kabeer, the Immaculate Lord is indescribable. ||2||2||11||
Gazing upon the diamond, I humbly salute this diamond.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਗਮ ਹੁਸੀਆਰ ਪਹਰੂਆ ਦੇਖਤ ਜਮੁ ਲੇ ਜਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: nigam huseeaar paharooaa dekhat jam le jaaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: While the Vedas stand guard watching, the Messenger of Death carries you away. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਨੀਆ ਹੁਸੀਆਰ ਬੇਦਾਰ ਜਾਗਤ ਮੁਸੀਅਤ ਹਉ ਰੇ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: duneea huseeaar bedaar jaagat museeat hau re bhaaee |
lines.english: People of the world, remain awake and aware. Even though you are awake, you are being robbed, O Siblings of Destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੀੰਬੁ ਭਇਓ ਆਂਬੁ ਆਂਬੁ ਭਇਓ ਨੀੰਬਾ ਕੇਲਾ ਪਾਕਾ ਝਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: neenb bheo aanb aanb bheo neenbaa kelaa paakaa jhaar |
lines.english: He thinks that the bitter nimm fruit is a mango, and the mango is a bitter nimm. He imagines the ripe banana on the thorny bush.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਲੀਏਰ ਫਲੁ ਸੇਬਰਿ ਪਾਕਾ ਮੂਰਖ ਮੁਗਧ ਗਵਾਰ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naaleer fal sebar paakaa moorakh mugadh gavaar |1|
lines.english: He thinks that the ripe coconut hangs on the barren simmal tree; what a stupid, idiotic fool he is! ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਭਇਓ ਖਾਂਡੁ ਰੇਤੁ ਮਹਿ ਬਿਖਰਿਓ ਹਸਤੀੰ ਚੁਨਿਓ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: har bheo khaandd ret meh bikhario hasateen chunio na jaaee |
lines.english: The Lord is like sugar, spilled onto the sand; the elephant cannot pick it up.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਿ ਕਮੀਰ ਕੁਲ ਜਾਤਿ ਪਾਂਤਿ ਤਜਿ ਚੀਟੀ ਹੋਇ ਚੁਨਿ ਖਾਈ ॥੨॥੩॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: keh kameer kul jaat paant taj cheettee hoe chun khaaee |2|3|12|
lines.english: Says Kabeer, give up your ancestry, social status and honor; be like the tiny ant - pick up and eat the sugar. ||2||3||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਣੀ ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਜੀਉ ਕੀ ਰਾਮਕਲੀ ਘਰੁ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: baanee naamadeo jeeo kee raamakalee ghar 1 |
lines.english: The Word Of Naam Dayv Jee, Raamkalee, First House:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਨੀਲੇ ਕਾਗਦੁ ਕਾਟੀਲੇ ਗੂਡੀ ਆਕਾਸ ਮਧੇ ਭਰਮੀਅਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: aaneele kaagad kaatteele gooddee aakaas madhe bharameeale |
lines.english: The boy takes paper, cuts it and makes a kite, and flies it in the sky.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਚ ਜਨਾ ਸਿਉ ਬਾਤ ਬਤਊਆ ਚੀਤੁ ਸੁ ਡੋਰੀ ਰਾਖੀਅਲੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: panch janaa siau baat btaooaa cheet su ddoree raakheeale |1|
lines.english: Talking with his friends, he still keeps his attention on the kite string. ||1||
My mind has been pierced by the Name of the Lord,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਨਿਕ ਕਲਾ ਚਿਤੁ ਮਾਂਡੀਅਲੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jaise kanik kalaa chit maanddeeale |1| rahaau |
lines.english: like the goldsmith, whose attention is held by his work. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਨੀਲੇ ਕੁੰਭੁ ਭਰਾਈਲੇ ਊਦਕ ਰਾਜ ਕੁਆਰਿ ਪੁਰੰਦਰੀਏ ॥
lines.translit: aaneele kunbh bharaaeele aoodak raaj kuaar purandaree |
lines.english: The young girl in the city takes a pitcher, and fills it with water.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਸਤ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਕਰਤੀ ਹੈ ਚੀਤੁ ਸੁ ਗਾਗਰਿ ਰਾਖੀਅਲੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: hasat binod beechaar karatee hai cheet su gaagar raakheeale |2|
lines.english: She laughs, and plays, and talks with her friends, but she keeps her attention focused on the pitcher of water. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਦਰੁ ਏਕੁ ਦੁਆਰ ਦਸ ਜਾ ਕੇ ਗਊ ਚਰਾਵਨ ਛਾਡੀਅਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: mandar ek duaar das jaa ke gaoo charaavan chhaaddeeale |
lines.english: The cow is let loose, out of the mansion of the ten gates, to graze in the field.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਂਚ ਕੋਸ ਪਰ ਗਊ ਚਰਾਵਤ ਚੀਤੁ ਸੁ ਬਛਰਾ ਰਾਖੀਅਲੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: paanch kos par gaoo charaavat cheet su bachharaa raakheeale |3|
lines.english: It grazes up to five miles away, but keeps its attention focused on its calf. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਕਾਜ ਬਿਰੂਧੀ ਚੀਤੁ ਸੁ ਬਾਰਿਕ ਰਾਖੀਅਲੇ ॥੪॥੧॥
lines.translit: antar baahar kaaj biroodhee cheet su baarik raakheeale |4|1|
lines.english: Its mother is at work, inside and outside, but she holds her child in her thoughts. ||4||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹਤ ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਸੁਨਹੁ ਤਿਲੋਚਨ ਬਾਲਕੁ ਪਾਲਨ ਪਉਢੀਅਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: kehat naamadeo sunahu tilochan baalak paalan paudteeale |
lines.english: Says Naam Dayv, listen, O Trilochan: the child is laid down in the cradle.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੇਦ ਪੁਰਾਨ ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਆਨੰਤਾ ਗੀਤ ਕਬਿਤ ਨ ਗਾਵਉਗੋ ॥
lines.translit: bed puraan saasatr aanantaa geet kabit na gaavaugo |
lines.english: There are countless Vedas, Puraanas and Shaastras; I do not sing their songs and hymns.
